# priority: high
# category: bugs
** FIXED #128 Auto-generated enum clone in ~initer.til~ is shallow for managed payloads

Post-mortem: the shallow clone described below was fixed as part of the
issue #109 payload-ownership work: ~generate_enum_clone_method~ now
computes ~payload_needs_clone~ (non-primitive, non-Dynamic, non-funcsig
payloads) and wraps the extracted payload ref in a ~.clone()~ call
before passing it to the variant constructor (initer.til, "Issue #109"
comment in ~generate_enum_clone_method~). The variant ctor takes the
payload as ~own~ for compound types, so the cloned enum owns a fresh
deep copy and the auto-generated delete (#126) can free both copies
independently -- no aliasing, no double-free. This issue stayed open
because no regression test pinned the behaviour; this commit adds
~test_str_payload_clone_deep~, ~test_struct_payload_clone_deep~ and
~test_nested_enum_payload_clone_deep~ to test/enums.til, which clone,
mutate the original through a ref-mut payload binding, and assert the
clone kept the pre-mutation payload (the ASAN suite would also catch
any double-free from a shared payload buffer).

The delete side of this issue is fixed ([[./126.org][#126]], now in [[../fixed/][issues/fixed/]]):
  ~generate_enum_delete_method~ recurses into payload-owned data.

  Remaining problem: auto-generated enum ~clone~ does NOT deep-clone
  managed payloads. In ~generate_enum_clone_method~ (initer.til:668),
  the payload is declared with ~own_type = OwnType.Ref~ and passed
  directly to the constructor without calling ~.clone()~. This means
  the cloned enum shares the same payload data as the original --
  a shallow copy instead of a deep clone.

  This matters for payload enums carrying managed values like:
  - ~Str~
  - structs with owned internals
  - containers such as ~Vec~

  Verified examples already allowed by the language/tests:
  - ~Token.Name: Str~
  - ~Shape.Dot: Point~
  - ~Shape.Line: Str~

  Desired end state:
  - auto-generated enum ~clone~ calls ~PayloadType.clone()~ on managed
    payload data before passing it to the constructor
  - no double-free or aliasing from cloned enums sharing payload buffers
